source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The new TV screens have the sides $16: 9$ and the old ones have the sides 4:3.
<image1>
We have a DVD that occupies exactly all the screen 16:9. We want to watch this film on the old 4:3 screen. If the width of the film occupies exactly the width of the old screen, then the empty part of the screen is:

choice_map.E: It depends on the size of the screen

image_dependency: Question is fully understandable from the text alone using aspect ratios 16:9 and 4:3
An image asset exists in the registry, but no visual detail is referenced in the question text
multi_step: Requires combining two aspect ratios (16:9 film and 4:3 screen)
Need to infer that matching widths implies different heights, then determine the unused screen portion
This is more than direct lookup and involves at least a short sequence of ratio/area reasoning
verifiability: Multiple-choice options are provided, giving a clear target answer
The final answer is a precise fraction, which is easy to check computationally

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
The problem is fully understandable from the text alone, so core semantic content is recoverable and stable
High verifiability score and short precise answer make the sample easy to check
Moderate multi-step reasoning value supports inclusion despite low image dependency
The only flagged risk is that the image is not needed, which does not prevent successful use of the sample
